macro_segments.title: Rabbi Shimon's Distinction: Refusing Intermarriage Only in Cases of Definite Prohibition, Not Uncertainty

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan ben Nuri's Proposal: Creating Consensus Through Mandatory Chalitzah

micro_segments.title: The Question: How Should the Halacha Spread in Israel—According to Beit Shammai or Beit Hillel?
The Problem: Each Position Creates Mamzerim or Issur According to the Other House
The Solution: Require All Cases to Perform Chalitzah, Satisfying Both Houses
The Failed Consensus: The Process Was Never Completed ('Lo Hispiku Ligmor')

macro_segments.title: Rabban Gamliel's Objection: The Human and Retroactive Costs of Imposing New Standards

micro_segments.title: The Concern: What About Earlier Tzarot Who Already Performed Yibbum Without Chalitzah?
If Beit Shammai Had Been Following Beit Hillel All Along, There Would Be No Retroactive Problem
The Real Concern: The Tzara Herself, Not the Children, Would Be Rendered Forbidden
The Human Cost: Requiring Chalitzah Retroactively Damages Women's Status and Dignity ('Mi Masya Gavreihu')
The Principle: 'Darcheha Darchei Noam'—Torah's Ways Are Pleasantness, Not Playing It Safe at Human Expense
